sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **NemoClaw (NVIDIA)** — Announced at GTC 2026. An enterprise security stack that wraps OpenClaw with audit logging, access control, and guardrails. Positioned to normalize OpenClaw's ecosystem scale (346K stars, 44K skills, 50+ platforms) for enterprise adoption.

citations.label: The New Stack - Persistent AI Agents Compared

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent (Nous Research)** — "The agent that grows with you." Centers on a closed-loop learning model that autonomously generates, refines, and accumulates skills across sessions. Positioned at zero-CVE and serverless $5/mo. Six platform integrations (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, email).
**Framing comparison** — OpenClaw is "breadth of integration"; Hermes is "depth of learning." OpenClaw lacks a native skill-learning layer and treats every problem as new.

**v0.18 / v0.19 (April 2026)** — Two majors within a few weeks. gRPC serving, GPU-side migration of NGram speculative decoding with async-scheduler support, selective KV-cache CPU offload (FlexKV backend added), Day-1 Gemma 4 support, and important security patches.
**Claude Opus 4.7 GA** — Improvements to agentic coding, long-horizon tasks, and instruction following. Available on Bedrock.

citations.label: AWS - Claude Opus 4.7 now available in Amazon Bedrock

bullets.body: **Claude Mythos Preview (Gated Research Preview)** — A specialized preview that reads large codebases and identifies vulnerabilities and exploitability.
**Per-IAM-principal cost attribution** — CUR 2.0 / Cost Explorer can now break down inference cost by IAM user or role.
**Structured Outputs extended to GovCloud (US)** — GA across all commercial and GovCloud regions.
**AWS Agent Registry** — Mentioned together in the April 13 weekly.
**Open-weight Qwen3.6-35B-A3B** — Runs locally on consumer laptops. A 2026-04-17 review reports it beating Claude Opus 4.7 on visual-reasoning tasks.

citations.label: n1n.ai - Qwen3.6 vs Claude 4.7 Local LLM Review

bullets.body: **PrismML Bonsai 8B** — Native 1-bit training (every weight is -1/+1). 8B fits in 1.15 GB, 14x smaller than 16-bit, 4-5x energy efficiency. iPhone 17 Pro Max measured at 0.068 mWh/tok.
**Bonsai 1.7B + WebGPU** — Compressed to 290 MB; in-browser GPU inference demos spread (2026-04-16). The same writeup also covers Ollama multi-agent and Gemma4 26B.

**b8838 (2026-04-18)** — Adds `llama-cli --endpoint` for remote-server connection, a prefix-cache fix for the Anthropic API, request-handling improvements for WebUI thinking mode, and a **verifiable inference sample** using SHA-256 / Merkle trees (commit-and-open).
**OpenVINO backend (preview)** — Optimized inference for Intel CPU / GPU / NPU.

**v0.20.0 breaks Gemma 4 tool calling** — Parser failures and dropped tool calls under streaming reported (April 2026). v0.6.2 (March) shipped Llama 4 support, batch-embedding API, Flash Attention v2.7, and M4 Metal 3 optimization.

sections.category: Ecosystems (local inference runtimes) > Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: **On-device inference solution** — Strong on hardware optimization and enterprise workflow integration. Continues to draw comparison articles as the alternative position to Ollama.

citations.label: Knowledge Share - Mastering Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: **CIS report (April 2026)** — Warns that prompt injection is a serious and growing threat to organizations using generative AI.

citations.label: CIS Press Release - Prompt Injection Attacks

bullets.body: **Attack trends** — Attempts +340% YoY (Q4 2025), successes +190%. Direct injection is under 20% of cases; over 80% are indirect (web, email, document-mediated).
**Government-use advisory** — Prompt injection is leaking into routine workflows.
**CVE-2026-33826** — Windows Active Directory improper input validation (CWE-20). An authenticated attacker can RCE via a crafted RPC call. CVSS **8.0**.
**CVE-2026-32157** — Remote Desktop Client critical RCE. CVSS **8.8**.
**CVE-2026-33827** — Windows TCP/IP critical RCE. CVSS **8.1**.

sections.category: Bay Area / Silicon Valley AI Meetups

bullets.body: **TechCon Silicon Valley 2026** (2026-04-06, Moscone Center South, SF) — AI track included.
**Generative AI Summit** (2026-04-15, DoubleTree by Hilton San Jose).
**Silicon Valley AI Innovators / SF Bay Area AI Meetup** — Image Generation and Understanding (2026-04-23), Zero-Shot Coreset Selection (2026-04-30), and others on the calendar.
**Bond AI - SF and Bay Area** (Luma calendar).
**AI Tinkerers SF**.
**Simon Willison (2026-04-18)** — A post on system prompts. Shares a one-shot example where a short prompt produced an outsized result.
**Qwen3.6-Plus (proprietary cloud)** — Alibaba positions this as their "first true agentic LLM," a proprietary tier above the open weights. Access is restricted to the chatbot site and Alibaba Cloud.

citations.label: Medium - Qwen3.6-Plus: The First Real Agentic LLM?

bullets.body: **Claude Opus 4.6 tops LMSYS Chatbot Arena** — Beats GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro on human-preference scores. SWE-bench Verified hits 65.3%, a new bar for agentic coding.

**EU AI Act in full effect (March 2026)** — High-risk AI applications now require log retention and conformity assessment. [Build Fast With AI - Best AI Models April 2026](https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/best-ai-models-april-2026) ---

citations.label: Build Fast With AI - Best AI Models April 2026
